chapter_question: How is a curve on a surface split into normal and geodesic turning?
inspection_targets: A geodesic on a surface of revolution keeps r sin(alpha) fixed.
The total curvature vector splits into normal and tangential pieces.
Normal sections compare projected curvature values.

source_span.focus: Separates normal and geodesic curvature and develops geodesics on surfaces of revolution through Clairaut and Kepler analogies.
source_span.label: Chapter 11
source_span.title: Geodesics and Geodesic Curvature
topics: Intrinsic and extrinsic measurements of geodesic curvature
Clairaut's theorem on surfaces of revolution
Kepler's second law and hyperbolic geodesics
